fields.note: Need Callable for transform_callback type hint, keeping existing Sequence import

Merged version with both strip_metadata and transform_callback params. methods normalized to a tuple/sequence and applied in a loop; strip_metadata clears exif_image.info and exif after transpose+orientation-tag removal, covering all branches (in_place/not, transposed/not). Please review & compare byte-for-byte.

Close to your draft, kept your param order (transform_callback then strip_metadata) and docstring style. Two correctness fixes: (1) strip_metadata now applies unconditionally, even when orientation=1/no transpose happens (both in_place and not in_place) - otherwise a very common case (no rotation needed) silently keeps all metadata despite strip_metadata=True. (2) use exif_image.getexif().clear() in addition to info={} - just clearing .info leaves a stale cached self._exif (already _loaded=True from the earlier 'image_exif = image.getexif()' call) that would still expose the old tags if .getexif() is called again on the same object, especially relevant for in_place=True where exif_image is the same object. Also added '| None' to the transform_callback return type since None is a valid/handled return already. LMK if you agree, otherwise send adjustments.
Confirmed - adopting this exact text verbatim (your latest version). Functionally equivalent to what I proposed, just structured slightly differently. Locking this in for Phase 3.
